## AI that remembers and forgets like humans.

115 followers

## AI that remembers and forgets like humans.

115 followers

Visit website

AI Infrastructure Tools

Most agents are either amnesiacs or "hoarders" that choke on stale context and break their own reasoning. YourMemory brings biological logic to the workflow. Using the Ebbinghaus curve, it prunes the junk so only the important stuff sticks. -84% Token Waste: Leaner context, sharper reasoning. 52% Recall: (LoCoMo benchmarked). v1.3.0 Graph Engine: Finds what you forgot to ask for. 100% Local.

YourMemoryp/yourmemorysachit mishra•

2mo ago [...] I decided to try a biological approach: YourMemory is an MCP server that uses the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve to manage context. It reinforces what matters and prunes the noise, just like we do.  
The highlights:

Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works Everywhere

Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works EverywhereStop typing. Start speaking. 4x faster.

Promoted

sachit mishra

YourMemory

YourMemory

Maker

📌 [...] Hey everyone, I’m Sachit.  
I built YourMemory because I hit a wall with my own coding workflow. My agents were brilliant, but their memory was a mess. They either forgot my architectural 'gotchas' by lunch, or they got so bogged down in stale bug fixes from last week that they started hallucinating.  
  
I realized we don't need a digital filing cabinet for our agents, we need a filter.  
YourMemory treats context as a living thing. It uses 'biological decay' to let transient noise fade away while reinforcing the patterns and facts you actually use.  
  
For the skeptics:

results.title: TxGemma: AI Models for Faster Drug Development | Product Hunt
results.content:
Key aspects:  
  
💊 Therapeutics Focus: Tailored for drug discovery and related biological tasks.  
🧬 Gemma 2 Base: Leverages the efficient Gemma 2 architecture.  
🧪 Fine-Tunable: Includes a Colab notebook showing how to adapt it to specific therapeutic data or tasks.

🤖 Agent Ready: Can be integrated as a tool within larger AI agent systems (demonstrated with Agentic-Tx).     
⚖️ Open Weights (Gemma License): Available on Hugging Face and Vertex AI, allowing broad research and development (check license terms).     
  
This will be a powerful and accessible toolkit for researchers and developers in biotech, pharma, and computational biology.

Jun Shen

This will speed up drug development! 😄 [...] TxGemma

Flowtica Scribe

Flowtica Scribe [...] Zac Zuo
Flowtica Scribe

Flowtica Scribe

Hi everyone!  
  
Sharing TxGemma, a new family of open models from Google DeepMind, built on Gemma 2 and specifically designed to accelerate therapeutics development. This aims to tackle the slow, expensive drug discovery process using specialized AI.     
  
TxGemma comes in two flavors and multiple sizes (2B, 9B, 27B):

TxGemma-Predic focuses on tasks like predicting molecule properties (toxicity, binding affinity, etc.) based on Therapeutic Data Commons benchmarks. The 27B version shows impressive performance, rivaling specialized models.

TxGemma-Chat, on the other hand, adds conversational abilities, allowing you to ask why it made a prediction or discuss complex therapeutic data.

results.title: Socra: From Curiosity to Mastery: Socrates-Powered Learning | Product Hunt
results.content:
\ Goal-Oriented Dialogue: At the start of each session, Socra establishes a specific objective. It then guides the conversation around this goal, ensuring the Socratic dialogue remains focused without drifting off-topic.

\ Rich Post-Conversation Artifacts: After the discussion, Socra generates valuable "Artifacts," such as memos or blog posts. This transforms the dialogue into a structured, organized format that is easy to review and reflect upon.

Sviatoslav Dvoretskii [...] Socra

Socra
Wood Peng

3mo ago [...] Picture this: you hit a wall on a problem, ask an AI for help, get a clean answer, and move on. Efficient, right?

Maybe. But a week later, you can't remember a thing. The AI solved your problem but your brain never engaged with it. Over time, this habit quietly chips away at your ability to think independently.

Socra takes a fundamentally different approach.
